key_decisions.reasoning: All three examples are in the strictOffset() Javadoc and should demonstrate strictOffset() behavior; the non-strict offset() calls are simply wrong in context

solve_notes: The Javadoc examples in `strictOffset()` incorrectly used `offset()` (non-strict factory) in all three sample assertions. With non-strict offset(0.1), `|8.1-8.0|=0.1 <= 0.1` would pass — contradicting the claimed failure. Fixed by replacing all three `offset()` calls with `strictOffset()` in the Javadoc. Also added a clarifying inline comment on the `areEqual` early-return in `Numbers.assertIsCloseTo` to explain why it is correct for strict offsets: when actual==expected the diff is 0, and 0 < any positive strict offset value, so returning early (passing) is correct.
task_text:
Fix Offset.strictOffset Javadoc examples using wrong factory method and Numbers.assertIsCloseTo wrong file reference

In `assertj-core/src/main/java/org/assertj/core/data/Offset.java`, the
`strictOffset` method's Javadoc code examples mistakenly call `offset()`
(non-strict factory) in all three sample assertions instead of
`strictOffset()`, making the documented failure cases incorrect. For
example, `assertThat(8.1).isCloseTo(8.0, offset(0.1))` would pass
(non-strict `<=`), not fail as the example claims. Additionally,
`internal/Comparables.java` is sometimes incorrectly referenced for
`assertIsCloseTo` — the actual implementation lives in
`internal/Numbers.java`. Fix the Javadoc examples in `Offset.java` to use
`strictOffset()` and add a clarifying comment in
`Numbers.assertIsCloseTo` explaining why the early-return `areEqual` guard
correctly passes for strict offsets when `diff == 0` (since `0 < any positive strictOffset`).

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: calls strictOffset() directly and verifies it creates a strict Offset with correct value, exercising the strictOffset factory method that had the wrong Javadoc
exercises the areEqual early-return path in Numbers.assertIsCloseTo (when actual==expected), covering the line whose comment was clarified
exercises the areEqual early-return path in Numbers.assertIsCloseTo (when actual==expected), covering the line whose comment was clarified

test_selection.test_query: Find tests that verify the behavior of strictOffset and assertIsCloseTo in assertj-core/src/main/java/org/assertj/core/data/Offset.java and assertj-core/src/main/java/org/assertj/core/internal/Numbers.java

thrash_preventing_defs.name: offset
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: need to understand non-strict offset semantics (<=) to confirm the strictOffset Javadoc examples are wrong and to correctly replace offset() with strictOffset() in examples

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers exactly the two defs changed. The thrash_preventing set adds the non-strict `offset()` factory method, because an AI agent implementing this task needs to understand what `offset()` does (non-strict, `<=`) to recognize why the existing Javadoc examples are wrong and how to fix them. Without reading `offset()`, the agent might be confused about which calls to replace or whether the examples were actually wrong.
